sourceMovie.title: Last Man Standing
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Last Man Standing (1996) stuck because it commits to its crime premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Walter Hill's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The crime and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: No Country for Old Men
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Last Man Standing clicked for you, No Country for Old Men (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and western, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Scarface
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Last Man Standing clicked for you, Scarface (1983) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Last Man Standing?
faq.answer: Try No Country for Old Men, Scarface, Lawless—each shares DNA with Last Man Standing while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Last Man Standing?
faq.answer: Last Man Standing (1996) is best described as Crime, Action, Western. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Last Man Standing worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
